content.method: Comparative case-study synthesis of Finnish, Swedish, and Polish post-reform choice/zoning policy variants matched against segregation trajectories; simulate Hungarian-context counterfactuals using existing school-choice and commuting pattern data.
content.question: What minimum institutional safeguards (funding equalization, curricular parity, teacher placement rules, zoning/choice controls) are necessary to prevent re-segregation through school choice and within-school stratification if formal early tracking is removed, as observed in Finland and Sweden post-1990s?
content.why_it_matters: T2 and T6 explicitly depend on this; without paired safeguards, de-tracking risks the Finnish/Swedish failure mode of choice-driven resegregation, undermining the core rationale for structural reform.
